content:
# Stratigraphy and Historical Geology

**Summary**: Broad research theme addressed in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] contributed to the theme of Stratigraphy

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] conducted a stratigraphical analysis of the Jabal Hafeet succession.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] used stratigraphy to define the sequence of the Thamama Group.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] applied stratigraphy to define the lithostratigraphic boundaries of the Cambrian-Ordovician succession.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] analyzed the stratigraphic architecture of syn-rift carbonate platforms.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] applied stratigraphy to determine the relationship between different lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] focused on stratigraphy to establish a framework for the Paleocene formations.

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] applied high-resolution stratigraphy to define the Upper Khartam Member's architecture.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] performed stratigraphical analysis to define the sequence of the Late Cretaceous rocks.

## Key Subtopics



- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] conducted a stratigraphical analysis of the Jabal Hafeet succession.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] used stratigraphy to define the sequence of the Thamama Group.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] applied stratigraphy to define the lithostratigraphic boundaries of the Cambrian-Ordovician succession.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] analyzed the stratigraphic architecture of syn-rift carbonate platforms.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] applied stratigraphy to determine the relationship between different lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] focused on stratigraphy to establish a framework for the Paleocene formations.

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] applied high-resolution stratigraphy to define the Upper Khartam Member's architecture.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] performed stratigraphical analysis to define the sequence of the Late Cretaceous rocks.

## Methods and Approaches Used



## Key Findings Across Sources

- contributed to the theme of Stratigraphy ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]])

- Identified five distinct microfacies to interpret the age and paleoenvironmental conditions. ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992, p. 13]])

- Stratigraphic units were correlated across the basin using a combination of well-log data and core analysis. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 23]])

- The study established a clear distinction between the Siq, Quweira, and Saq Sandstone units. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 42]])

- Identified aggradational to progradational shallowing upward parasequences in the platform margin. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 62]])

- Not stated in source ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]])

- Established a sequence stratigraphic framework including super-sequences, sequences, and depositional units. ([[2016-awad|Awad, 2016, p. 17]])

- High-frequency sequences (HFS) were identified as key units for understanding reservoir scale. ([[2017-adam|Adam, 2017, p. 130]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]])

- Identified three distinct formations: Gudair, Hartha, and Qurna. ([[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989, p. 12]])
